maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday October 17, 2023 at 02:00

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is unprocessed. Contact the Medical Historical Library to request access.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred to Medical Historical Library by William Summers, 2023.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Franklin Hutchinson (1920-1997) was a biophysicist with an interest in radiation biology and mathematics. He earned a bachelor's degree in physics from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) in 1942 then joined the Radiation Laboratory at MIT. He earned three patents in microwave electronics for his work on the development of radar during World War II. In 1945 he began graduate studies in physics at Yale, completing a PhD in 1948, then joining the biophysics program faculty. In 1960 he became professor of biophysics in the newly formed department of molecular biophysics and biochemistry, later serving as department chair and director of graduate and undergraduate studies. Hutchinson taught at Yale until 1990 and continued his research as Emeritus Professor until his death.
Hutchinson joined the Radiation Research Society in 1953 and published regularly in Radiation Research . He was a member of the American Physical Society and the International Union of Biophysics.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Franklin Hutchinson papers. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Papers include materials created or collected by Franklin Hutchinson (1920-1997) during his work in Yale's biophysics and biochemistry departments from 1973-1997. Materials include correspondence, a report on the health effects of low frequency electromagnetics, department and committee work, including investigations into the authenticity of the Vinland Map, and photographs.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.
